explanation: Inline assembly bypasses the Solidity type system, overflow checks, and memory safety guarantees, giving raw access to the EVM. Mistakes in assembly (wrong memory offsets, unchecked arithmetic, clobbered slots) are invisible to the compiler and to most tooling.
impact: A single wrong offset or unchecked operation in an assembly block can corrupt storage, leak or destroy funds, and slip past reviews and static analyzers that assume Solidity-level safety.
